issues.observed_behavior: A direct two-peer landing request could still escalate into a bridge summary even when a plain local reply would feel more natural.
issues.expected_behavior: Stay with a normal peer reply unless the room truly needs a bridge-level intervention.
issues.rationale: Live-room realism drops when Orion starts synthesizing the whole room for a tight local disagreement.

issues.observed_behavior: Repair grounding used managerial wording that sounded like Orion was moving participants around the room.
issues.expected_behavior: Repair wording should read like a quick course correction and follow the room's correction without sounding supervisory.
issues.rationale: Even correct repair decisions can feel off if the grounding text sounds like room control instead of repair.

issues.observed_behavior: Episode snapshots and re-entry anchors could stay softly present even while a live clarifying question already dominated the thread.
issues.expected_behavior: When the room is actively clarifying, resumptive cues should step out of the way so the live ambiguity stays primary.
issues.rationale: Resumptive cues are useful background, but they become noise when the room is already working through a live clarification boundary.

issues.observed_behavior: GIFs could stay too rare in clearly playful rooms unless the peer explicitly used one first.
issues.expected_behavior: A fresh playful room with enough text-only runway can allow an occasional bounded GIF even without a peer GIF trigger.
issues.rationale: Under-firing in playful rooms makes Orion feel oddly flat and less socially natural.

issues.observed_behavior: A medium-chaos room could still receive another GIF even when thread churn was already rising.
issues.expected_behavior: In medium-chaos rooms, Orion should keep GIF use rarer and fall back to plain text sooner.
issues.rationale: Over-firing expressive media in an already busy room makes the room feel more chaotic than grounded.

issues.observed_behavior: The same GIF intent could loop back onto the same peer after only a short gap.
issues.expected_behavior: Recent intent loops should suppress another GIF so Orion does not get stuck repeating the same gag.
issues.rationale: Repeated GIF intent loops read as mechanical and can quickly become socially awkward.

issues.observed_behavior: Older ritual/style hints could still nudge Orion toward a GIF even when fresher caution context said to stay text-first.
issues.expected_behavior: Stale ritual hints should not outweigh fresh caution context or freshness warnings.
issues.rationale: Fresh context should outrank stale vibe hints so GIF selection remains grounded in the live room.

issues.observed_behavior: Transport fallback to text-only had too little replay coverage and could regress without clear harvesting evidence.
issues.expected_behavior: When media hints are unsupported, the reply should stay text-only and omit GIF transport metadata.
issues.rationale: Fallback behavior is easy to overlook unless the replay harness keeps harvesting it explicitly.

issues.observed_behavior: A clearly-labeled celebratory GIF could still be treated as an opaque blob.
issues.expected_behavior: Clear title/query metadata should yield a conservative celebration cue.
issues.rationale: When metadata is strong, Orion should notice the likely social reaction without pretending vision.

issues.observed_behavior: Thin GIF metadata could be overclaimed as a specific reaction.
issues.expected_behavior: Weak metadata should stay unknown and low-confidence.
issues.rationale: The proxy must be explicit about uncertainty instead of fabricating visual confidence.

issues.observed_behavior: Nearby peer text was not helping Orion disambiguate an otherwise generic GIF.
issues.expected_behavior: Surrounding text can lightly disambiguate the likely reaction when metadata alone is generic.
issues.rationale: A bounded text proxy should use adjacent conversational text when it genuinely helps.

issues.observed_behavior: A plausible peer-GIF reaction could still pull too much weight in a contested clarification turn.
issues.expected_behavior: Contested or repair-heavy turns should keep the GIF cue secondary or ignored.
issues.rationale: Live text, repair, and epistemic caution must outrank the GIF proxy.

issues.observed_behavior: Low-signal tag/title data could be overfit into a reaction that looked more certain than the metadata warranted.
issues.expected_behavior: Conflicting or low-signal title/tag data should stay uncertain.
issues.rationale: The proxy should remain conservative around noisy metadata.

issues.observed_behavior: A transport-level GIF presence signal could still look more informative than it really was when no useful proxy text existed.
issues.expected_behavior: No useful metadata should still register that a GIF happened while keeping the meaning unknown.
issues.rationale: Presence and meaning need to remain distinct in low-information transports.

fixes.summary: Lower bridge-summary eagerness for explicit landing requests when the thread is still a direct two-peer local exchange.
fixes.rationale: The room only needs a bridge when the disagreement is genuinely room-level, not just because someone asked where the local thread stands.

fixes.summary: Make repair grounding sound like a compact course correction instead of a retargeting command.
fixes.rationale: Repair can remain explicit without sounding like hidden room management.

fixes.summary: Exclude episode snapshots and re-entry anchors when a live clarifying/leave-open intervention is already steering the active thread.
fixes.rationale: Live uncertainty should outrank resumptive context when the room is still clarifying the current thread.

fixes.summary: Add a fresh playful-room bonus so bounded GIFs can appear after a text-only streak without requiring a peer GIF every time.
fixes.rationale: A playful room should feel lightly expressive, not artificially flattened.

fixes.summary: Tighten density blocking in medium-chaos rooms so GIFs back off before they add to room churn.
fixes.rationale: A busier room needs plainer delivery sooner.

fixes.summary: Detect recent same-intent / same-peer loops and suppress another GIF before the pattern becomes repetitive.
fixes.rationale: Occasional GIFs can feel natural; loops do not.

fixes.summary: Ignore stale ritual boosts and let fresh caution/freshness context keep the turn text-first.
fixes.rationale: Live context should outrank stale vibe memory.

fixes.summary: Expand replay harvesting so transport fallback stays visible and verified in GIF-specific regressions.
fixes.rationale: Fallback paths need the same regression visibility as allowed GIF paths.

fixes.summary: Extract bounded transport metadata and map clear celebratory evidence into a conservative peer-GIF reaction cue.
fixes.rationale: Clear metadata should help without pretending vision.

fixes.summary: Keep weak or generic GIF metadata unknown and high-ambiguity instead of inferring a precise reaction.
fixes.rationale: Uncertainty should remain visible when evidence is thin.

fixes.summary: Let nearby peer text lightly disambiguate the likely reaction when the GIF metadata itself is generic.
fixes.rationale: The proxy should use surrounding text, but still conservatively.

fixes.summary: Mark peer-GIF proxy cues as softened or ignored when live contested/repair cues are stronger.
fixes.rationale: The GIF proxy must stay subordinate to stronger live conversational cues.

fixes.summary: Keep noisy title/tag evidence low-confidence instead of overfitting it into a strong reaction claim.
fixes.rationale: The proxy should prefer uncertainty over invented precision.

fixes.summary: Separate GIF presence from GIF meaning so transport-only signals stay unknown when proxy text is absent.
